name: Behalf.bot — BFL (Body for Life)
description: Quantified-self pipeline for the Body for Life program. Ingests handwritten notebook photos (workout logs, meal logs, aerobic graphs) + food photos via cloud vision (Claude Opus + GPT-4o-mini), reconciles runs with Strava + Oura HR, enriches macros via USDA FoodData Central. Postgres-by-default via the chassis _chassis_db selector.

enabled.description: Master switch. If false the plugin is dormant — heartbeats not registered, triggers not dispatched.

archive_dir.description: Absolute path for raw photos, vision extractions, and unmatched food photos. Subdirectory layout: raw/, extractions/, unmatched/, ground-truth/, benchmark/. Local-only, never committed.

notebook_ingest.description: Enable the heartbeat that polls the health Discord channel for new notebook/food photos and runs vision-extract + ingest.

strava_oura_reconcile.description: Enable Strava activity polling + Oura HR reconciliation for runs. Requires STRAVA_* and OURA_TOKEN env vars sourced from the chassis secret store.

fdc_enrich.description: Enable USDA FoodData Central macro enrichment for food photos and description-only meals. Requires USDA_FDC_API_KEY (DEMO_KEY works at low volume). Default false - vision-primary is more accurate (see LESSONS_FROM_V1.md #36).

health_channel_id.description: Discord channel ID where the installer posts BFL photos and Backfill commands. Sourced from the chassis surface config; this field overrides if set.

health_channel_name.description: Human-readable channel name for confirmation messages and docs (e.g. installer-health).

ops_channel_name.description: Channel for failure escalations (e.g. installer-ops). Used when strava-ingest hits the consecutive-failure threshold.

daily_macro_targets.description: Default per-day macro targets per BFL methodology. Used by the briefing layer; not enforced by ingest.

hr_max.description: Max heart rate for Karvonen-style 0-10 intensity calculation. Tune per athlete.

hr_rest.description: Resting heart rate for Karvonen-style 0-10 intensity calculation. Tune per athlete.

database.description: Backend selector. 'postgres' is the canonical chassis backend; 'sqlite' is the legacy/local-dev fallback. Routed via plugins/bfl/scripts/_chassis_db.py.

activation._note: Activation gated on installer explicitly enabling per chassis.config.yaml modules.bfl.enabled. The plugin is dormant by default — quantified-self automation is opt-in.

_chassis_v1_notes.shell_implementation: Plugin ships Python scripts (bfl-*.py, strava-ingest.py, fdc_lookup.py, _chassis_db.py) plus a shell trigger handler stub. The 'register' semantics map: contracts.tools → installer can invoke plugins/bfl/scripts/<name>.py; contracts.heartbeats → bfl-ingest entry must be added to chassis/HEARTBEATS.md by the installer at activation time (see docs/plugins/bfl.md); contracts.triggers → registered with chassis dispatcher via #506 (this plugin's trigger handler is a stub that echoes its env vars until the parser library lands).
_chassis_v1_notes.compat_bundle: See .claude-plugin/plugin.json for the Claude Code compatibility shim.
_chassis_v1_notes.schema_migration: plugins/bfl/db/migrations/001_bfl.sql contains the 7 BFL tables (bfl_days, bfl_meals, bfl_workouts, bfl_runs, bfl_aerobic, bfl_meal_items, fdc_food_cache). The installer's bootstrap should psql this migration in after the chassis baseline schema lands.
_chassis_v1_notes.future_tables: bfl_food_corrections is referenced in skills/bfl.md as a future table for the correction-learning loop. NOT yet implemented in 001_bfl.sql — deliberately deferred.
